title: artemisia campestris subsp. pycnocephala
intro: Artemisia campestris subsp. pycnocephala, also known as Mugwort, is a perennial herb native to North America, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and bitter-tasting leaves. Traditionally, Mugwort has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and insomnia, due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mugwort include its ability to alleviate symptoms of menopause, improve digestion, and promote relaxation. In herbal preparations, Mugwort is often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
